summary.intent: Fix multiple CI failures in the moltis repo: first Rust clippy errors on Linux, then Playwright E2E test failures related to the Sandboxes page and send-document rendering.
summary.outcome: All three categories of failures were resolved: clippy cfg-gating issues were fixed, sandboxes E2E tests were fixed by mocking slow Apple Container API endpoints, and the send-document flaky test was stabilized with a longer timeout and more specific locator. All fixes were committed and pushed.

repo: The `apple` module in `crates/tools/src/sandbox/` is gated with `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]` but tests were importing it with `#[cfg(any(target_os = "macos", test))]`, causing Linux CI failures when --all-targets enables the test cfg.
CI runs on Linux, so macOS-only sandbox tests must be explicitly gated with `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]`.
Apple Container operations (listing containers, cleaning containers) are slow VM operations that can easily exceed E2E test timeouts — these endpoints should be mocked in UI behavior tests.
The Sandboxes page 'Refresh' button text changes to 'Loading…' during `fetchContainers()`, so tests looking for the exact text 'Refresh' fail if the initial fetch hasn't completed.
The settings page routing maps `/settings/sandboxes` using a prefix/suffix system in `router.js` where slashes become colons and then back.
E2E tests share server-side sessions (e.g., 'main') and accumulated tool call state across tests in the same file can affect later tests in the suite.

code.finding: The `use super::apple::*` import was gated with `cfg(any(target_os = "macos", test))` but the module declaration only has `cfg(target_os = "macos")`, causing Linux build failures with --all-targets.
`scope_name` and `property_args` methods on `CgroupSandbox` were private `fn` but tested from a submodule; fixed by making them `pub(crate)`.
The Refresh button text is `loadingContainers.value ? 'Loading…' : 'Refresh'`, so it won't be findable by exact name 'Refresh' until the initial container fetch completes.
Test was looking for 'Refresh' button before the initial container fetch completed; needed route mocking for /api/sandbox/containers to get fast response.
Third test in the file was flaky on CI — 5s timeout for `.document-container` was insufficient; increased to 10s and used more specific locator with the tool call ID.

workflow: When E2E tests fail only on CI but pass locally, the first suspects are: slower CI machines causing timeouts, and shared session state accumulation across tests in the same file.
Mocking slow backend API endpoints is the correct approach for E2E tests that verify UI behavior (button state, response shape) rather than actual backend operations.
YubiKey confirmation was required for git push, causing retry delays — git signing is enforced in this repo.

friction: The CI failure required multiple fix iterations: first fix introduced new failures (apple module functions now unreachable in tests), requiring a second round of diagnosis.
YubiKey tap required for git push caused a failed push attempt and manual retry loop.
The send-document flaky test required analyzing CI retry logs and error context diffs to distinguish the real root cause (slow rendering on CI) from a false lead (session state accumulation).
Reading CI logs required multiple bash tool calls and intermediate parsing steps to extract the relevant error messages.
open_items: The send-document test flakiness was mitigated with a longer timeout but the root cause (slow WebSocket event propagation on Linux CI) was not fully resolved — may recur.
The websocket.spec.js test at line 250 was observed as flaky during this session but was not fixed (deemed unrelated to current changes).
Shared server-side session state across E2E tests in the same file is a latent source of test pollution — tests could benefit from per-test session isolation.
